D9 Biology L4 Analyze Reference

Chlorophyll a vs b — Two Absorption Windows

chlorophyll a peaks at 430 + 662 nm; chlorophyll b at 453 + 642 nm — together they catch slightly different blue and red bands, and leave green to reflect

Green plants run two chlorophylls in parallel. Chlorophyll a is the reaction-centre pigment doing the actual electron-transfer; chlorophyll b is an accessory that catches photons in a slightly different band and funnels them to a. Their combined absorption covers the blue and red ends of the visible spectrum — the unaffected green light is what reflects back to your eye.

Toggle pigment to overlay each Soret + Q-band spectrum on the visible band. Both shown together produces the “green window” that leaves leaves green.
